key_insight: Glass-based physical models can mimic large, tissue-like deformations in Trichoplax adhaerens and reveal how cellular geometry changes under stretching.
engineering_relevance: The work offers a tunable, low-cost experimental platform for studying fracture-like tissue mechanics and comparing physical experiments with mechanical models.
method: Shape glass into tissue-like monolayers in a glass studio
Heat to the process temperature and apply controlled lateral or radial stretching
Rapidly cool to freeze the deformed state for imaging and analysis
evidence_signal: The experiments measured area and eccentricity changes in individual glass “cells,” and the tensegrity-based model reproduced the main deformation trends but underpredicted the eccentricity increase.
watch_item: The glass model captures qualitative behavior but may not fully match the magnitude of deformation observed in the biological system.
paper_title: Glass-based physical models for tissue mechanics
